EnterpriseSDK
Table of Contents

Interface ITin

Namespace
ESRI.ArcGIS.Geodatabase
Assembly
ESRI.Server.Geodatabase.dll

Provides access to members that control TINs.

[InterfaceType(ComInterfaceType.InterfaceIsIUnknown)]
[Guid("73B80FBB-FF33-11D1-A549-0000F8774F0F")]
public interface ITin

Properties

DataEdgeCount

The number of TIN edges that have a data triangle on one side or the other.

int DataEdgeCount { get; }

Property Value

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

DataNodeCount

The number of data nodes in the TIN.

int DataNodeCount { get; }

Property Value

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

DataTriangleCount

The number of data triangles in the TIN.

int DataTriangleCount { get; }

Property Value

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

Extent

The interpolation zone of the TIN.

IEnvelope Extent { get; }

Property Value

IEnvelope

Examples

Sample Snippet

Fields

The collection of attribute items of the TIN.

IFields Fields { get; }

Property Value

IFields

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<P>Each table in a database has an ordered collection of fields, there is always at least one field in a table. The ordered collection behaves like a list, so it is possible to access individual fields by a numbered position (or index) in the list.</P> <P>A <STRONG>Fields </STRONG>collection can also exist independently of a table, for example when creating an index on a table you will need to define which fields take part in the index. There are many interfaces which either require or return a <STRONG>Fields </STRONG>collection; these include <STRONG>IClass </STRONG>(therefore also <STRONG>IObjectClass </STRONG>and <STRONG>IFeatureClass</STRONG>), <STRONG>ICursor</STRONG>, <STRONG>IIndex</STRONG>, <STRONG>IFeatureClassDraw</STRONG>, <STRONG>IRowBuffer</STRONG>, <STRONG>ISimpleDataConverter</STRONG>, <STRONG>ITableSort </STRONG>and <STRONG>IValidate</STRONG>.</P>

HasEdgeTagValues

Indicates if the TIN dataset has edge tag values.

bool HasEdgeTagValues { get; }

Property Value

bool

Examples

Sample Snippet

HasNodeTagValues

Indicates if the TIN dataset has node tag values.

bool HasNodeTagValues { get; }

bool

HasTriangleTagValues

Indicates if the TIN dataset has triangle tag values.

bool HasTriangleTagValues { get; }

Property Value

bool

Examples

Sample Snippet

IsDelaunay

Indicates if TIN was constructed using Delaunay triangulation.

bool IsDelaunay { get; }

Property Value

bool

Examples

Sample Snippet

IsEmpty

Indicates if the TIN contains no data.

bool IsEmpty { get; }

Property Value

bool

Examples

Sample Snippet

Version

Returns the version number of the TIN engine used to build the TIN.

int Version { get; }

Property Value

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

ZFactor

Multiplication factor applied to all z values in a TIN to provide unit-congruency between coordinate components.

double ZFactor { get; set; }

Property Value

double

Examples

Sample Snippet

SaveAs(string, ref object)

Saves the TIN to disk using the specified name.

newName string
pOverWrite object
